title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is (0) RAPIDLY (RAPID) transforming the way we live and work. From (17) AUTOMATED (AUTOMATON) assistants to complex data analysis, its (18) APPLICATIONS (APPLY) are vast. While some fear the (19) DISPLACEMENT (PLACE) of workers, others argue that AI will create new (20) EMPLOYMENT (EMPLOY) opportunities in tech-driven sectors. The (21) ACCURACY (ACCURATE) of AI systems in medical diagnosis is already (22) IMPRESSIVE (IMPRESS). However, (23) ETHICAL (ETHICS) concerns regarding privacy and bias must be addressed (24) EFFECTIVELY (EFFECT). As we integrate AI into our daily routines, it is crucial to ensure that its development is aligned with human values. By fostering a collaborative approach between humans and machines, we can harness the power of technology to solve some of the world's most pressing challenges, ensuring a future where innovation serves the common good and enhances the quality of life for people everywhere, regardless of their background or location.

example.explanation: Adverb required to modify the verb 'transforming'; RAPID → RAPIDLY.

questions.explanation: Adjective (past participle) required to modify 'assistants'; AUTOMATON → AUTOMATED.
Noun with a prefix indicating removal/replacement required; PLACE → DISPLACEMENT.
Noun required to modify 'opportunities'; EMPLOY → EMPLOYMENT.
Noun required as the subject of the clause; ACCURATE → ACCURACY.
Adjective required after the verb 'is'; IMPRESS → IMPRESSIVE.
Adjective required to modify 'concerns'; ETHICS → ETHICAL.
Adverb required to modify the verb 'addressed'; EFFECT → EFFECTIVELY.
